outcome: Walking velocity (metres per second) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: Table 2: Speed (m/s) SUBAR Post 0.8+/-0.2, Control Post 0.8+/-0.3. Table 3: DSpeed 0.04+/-0.2 vs 0.07+/-0.1, P>.99

outcome: Lost to study during intervention phase, dropouts
source_quote: In the SUBAR group, 2 patients only completed 9 treatments... In the control group, all patients received 10 treatments. We finally analysed 30 patients, 15 patients in each group.

outcome: Walking velocity (metres per second) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: Table 4: 10MWT (m/s) E-RAGT Post-test 0.35+/-0.17, BWST Post-test 0.40+/-0.21. Within-group p: E-RAGT 0.024, BWST 0.019. ANOVA interaction p=0.162

outcome: Walking velocity (metres per second) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: increase in gait speed in the range from 0.48 to 0.81 m/s following I-Walk training... gait speed was significantly higher in the I-Walk than in the conventional PT at 6 and 8 weeks (F(1,28) = 5.9; p = 0.022; ES = 0.17). Baseline: I-Walk 0.48+/-0.19, Conventional PT 0.41+/-0.17

outcome: Walking capacity (metres walked in 6 minutes) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: both groups significantly improved in the 6 MWT (F(4,112) = 39.6; p < 0.001; ES = 0.59)... interaction effect of 6 MWT was found (F(4,112) = 4.0; p = 0.004; ES = 0.13). There was no group difference in 6 MWT (F(1,28) = 208.4; p = 0.38; ES = 0.03). Baseline: I-Walk 126.73+/-57.61, Conventional PT 116.33+/-55.69. improved 6 MWT distances of approximately 126-207 m
